ip_outcome_note: Card name matches species name. Art depicts a blue-bodied dragon with a vivid red face and prominent spines — canonical Druddigon design. PokemonTCG.io confirms card name 'Druddigon', National Pokedex no. 621, Unified Minds (sm11-157). Identity confirmed.

trivia_bullets.text: Druddigon is a pure Dragon-type from Generation V (Unova) and does not evolve from or into any other Pokemon — one of a small cohort of standalone fully-evolved Dragon-types in the franchise. National Pokedex no. 621 [PokemonTCG.io sm11-157; Bulbapedia: Druddigon, via WebSearch snippet].

Flavor text on this card (confirmed via PokemonTCG.io): 'The red skin on its face is harder than rock. In narrow caves, it squares off against its enemies and charges face-first right into them.' [PokemonTCG.io sm11-157]

Druddigon's design draws on the red-headed rock agama and European cave dragon archetypes, with gargoyle and troll references in its cold-weather immobility mechanic. Its red face is functionally hardened skin rather than conventional coloring — a design note the flavor text directly confirms [Bulbapedia: Druddigon, via WebSearch snippet; PokemonTCG.io sm11-157].

Druddigon's oracle text on this card includes Drag Off — an attack that forces an opponent's Benched Pokemon into the Active position before dealing damage — a mechanic that reflects its lore as a territorial ambush predator [PokemonTCG.io sm11-157 oracle text].

related_cards.name: Haxorus
related_cards.note: Fellow Unova Dragon — Druddigon and Haxorus are cited together as first fully evolved Dragon-types without a secondary type
